title: Cube CPU Cart
summary: A small rolling cube with two internal cubbies, sized to tuck a desktop computer tower out of the way — doubles as a compact end table.
description:
This is a small plywood cube divided by a center partition into two cubby columns, one of which gets a shelf partway up for smaller items, set on casters so a heavy desktop tower can be rolled out from under a desk instead of dragged. Building it small and square is what lets it double as an occasional end table if you ever repurpose it — the same reason to keep every joint tight even though most of this cube will be hidden under a desk.

Keep caster screws under 3/4" long. A screw that pokes through the bottom panel is a sharp point sitting on the floor, right where the cart rolls.

steps.title: Join two 1x8s
steps.body: Screw two of the 19-1/2"-long 1x8 boards together — either 2" countersunk screws with glue, or pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Add the cubby shelves
steps.body: Mark the inside faces of the boards from step 1, measuring down 6" from the top and up 6" from the bottom. Position the shelves at those marks and screw in place.

steps.title: Attach the center partition
steps.body: Attach the center partition to the cubby assembly.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body: Add one of the plywood panels to the back of the assembly.

steps.title: Attach top and bottom
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom panels to the shelves, keeping them flush to the shelf sides.

steps.title: Attach the final side
steps.body: Attach the last remaining side panel with glue and 2" screws or 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Add casters and finish
steps.body: Attach the four caster wheels to the bottom, using screws under 3/4" long so the tips don't poke through and out the inside bottom. Fill all holes with wood filler, let dry, sand, and finish as desired.

images.alt: Small rolling cube cart with two cubby compartments for a computer tower
